name: Golden Raisin Loaves
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large mixing bowl, stir together 1 3/4 cups whole wheat flour, sugar, undissolved yeast, salt, and cinnamon.
2. Heat water and butter in a saucepan over low heat to very warm (120-130 degrees F). Remove from the heat and stir in buttermilk.
3. Add the mixture to the flour-yeast combination and beat with an electric mixer on medium speed until smooth, about 2 minutes.
4. Beat in eggs for an additional 2 minutes, then stir in raisins. Gradually add remaining 1/4 cup whole wheat flour and enough white flour to form a stiff but light dough (heaviness indicates too much flour).
5. Let rest 5 minutes. Turn out onto lightly floured surface and knead until smooth, about 5 to 8 minutes.
6. Cover with bowl and let rise for 30 minutes. Punch down and divide in half.
7. Roll each half into a 12 X 7-inch rectangle. Starting at the 7-inch side, roll up tightly like a jelly roll and seal edges.
8. Tuck ends under to form rounded edges (loaf should be about 7 X 3-inches). Place on greased baking sheet.
9. Brush with oil and repeat for second batch of dough. Cover and let rise in warm place until almost doubled, about 30 minutes.
10. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F. Bake loaves for 25 minutes or until hollow when tapped.
11. Remove from baking sheet and cool on wire racks for 20 minutes. Frost with vanilla icing if desired.
VANILLA ICING: Combine sugar, vanilla, and milk in a bowl and beat until smooth.
